items.title: All shopping malls required to provide recycling facilities, says Nga
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: All shopping malls will be required to provide recycling facilities from this month as part of conditions for licence applications and renewals under local authorities. He said the initiative would begin with stakeholder engagement sessions and awareness programmes to encourage voluntary participation among mall operators, followed by pilot projects under selected local authorities to assess implementation effectiveness. The move aims to strengthen solid waste management, improve public access to recycling facilities and accelerate Malaysia's transition towards a circular economy.

items.title: Perlis Pas Muslimat chief apologises to MB, declines press secretary post
items.summary: KANGAR: Perlis Pas Muslimat chief Husna Hashim has publicly apologised to Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah for declining an appointment as his press secretary. Her decision came a day after the appointment was officially announced during the Perlis legislative assembly sitting. They involve the appointment of Perlis Pas Ulama Council chief Ahmad Adnan Fadzil as coordinator for the Guar Sanji constituency, Kangar Pas deputy chief Jamil Kamis as coordinator for the Bintong constituency, and Padang Besar Pas Education Committee chairman Abdul Aziz Ahmad as coordinator for the Chuping constituency.

items.title: Missing hiker Jaslinda found safe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Female hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing while trekking in the Gunung Batu Putih area in Ipoh, Perak, has been found safe this evening. Orang Asli volunteer coordinator Chew Ho Beng told BH the victim was found by villagers in the Lubuk Baharu Orang Asli settlement. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad, when contacted by BH, confirmed the discovery, with further details to follow.

items.title: ERL services suspended over technical issues, KLIA trains halted
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Express Rail Link (ERL) services were temporarily suspended today due to technical issues, affecting both KLIA Ekspres and KLIA Transit operations. In a service update on its official Facebook page, KLIA Ekspres said all train services had been halted until further notice. "Due to technical issues, all train services are temporarily suspended.

items.title: Negri Sembilan state election: Amanah's Bakri Sawir prepared to yield Klawang seat
items.summary: JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Amanah deputy chief Datuk Bakri Sawir has indicated his willingness to step aside in the upcoming state election, saying a successor has already been identified for the Klawang seat. He said this when met at a mass wedding ceremony for eight couples at Masjid Datuk Undang Luak Jelebu here today. Also present were Undang Luak Jelebu Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad, Jelebu member of parliament Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias, caretaker Sungai Lui assemblyman Datuk Mohd Razi Mohd Ali, and Jelebu Islamic religious officer and registrar of marriages, divorces and reconciliations (rujuk), Abdul Manas Md Isa.

items.title: GIIB founder tells court he faced ‘corporate mafia’ takeover plot and gun threat in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — GIIB Holdings Bhd founder Tai Boon Wee told the High Court that he was targeted in an alleged “corporate mafia” takeover attempt coordinated through a WhatsApp group known as “soldier boys”, and that he was also threatened with a gun at a restaurant, the New Straits Times (NST) reported. He told the court that a group involving Wong and Yee had discussed strategies to take over the listed company through the WhatsApp group. Tai added that the group also allegedly discussed delaying banking processes, manipulating media coverage and issuing negative statements aimed at depressing GIIB’s share price.

items.title: Hungry to bed: Unicef warns 72pct of Sabah children face food insecurity
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Support for families facing food insecurity in Sabah should be strengthened, according to a new report by the United Nations Children's Fund (Unicef). "More than one in three undocumented or stateless children experience extreme constraints on access to food," the report said. It also included 650 undocumented or stateless children and 274 children with disabilities (CWD).

items.title: [UPDATED] Saifuddin, Zaliha appointed PKR veeps
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail and Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa have been appointed PKR vice-presidents. PKR information chief Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the appointments were made following the party's Central Leadership Council monthly meeting in Batu Pahat, Johor, today. He said the venue would remain at the Melaka International Trade Centre (MITC) in Ayer Keroh, Melaka.

items.title: Two investigation papers involving Azam Baki referred to AGC, says Bukit Aman
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Two investigation papers involving former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki have been referred to the Attorney General’s Chambers (AGC) for further action. Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department (CID) director Datuk M. Kumar, in a statement today, said that investigations into the cases had been completed and the investigation papers were submitted to the AGC on Thursday (June 4). On Wednesday, Kumar said the police had recorded Azam’s statement under Section 112 of the Criminal Procedure Code to assist in the investigations.

items.title: Extra staff shore up understaffed Lahad Datu Hospital
items.summary: LAHAD DATU: Staff mobilisation and new placements will ensure uninterrupted healthcare services at Lahad Datu Hospital. The department said the strategy includes the mobilisation of medical officers through a cluster-based approach involving hospitals under the Kota Kinabalu and Tawau hospital clusters. The department said service capacity at the hospital would be strengthened with the placement of three permanent medical officers and two temporary medical officers.

items.title: King confers medals, honours on 107 recipients for official birthday
items.summary: According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, 7th Infantry Brigade Commander Brigadier-General Zulhelmi Masdar headed the list of recipients. Zulhelmi was awarded the Darjah Sultan Ibrahim Johor Yang Amat Disanjungi Pangkat Yang Kedua, Dato' Mulia Sultan Ibrahim Johor (DMIJ), which carries the title 'Datuk'. The honours list also included 46 recipients of the Pingat Sultan Ibrahim Pangkat Kedua and 13 recipients of the Pingat Sultan Ibrahim Pangkat Ketiga.

items.title: Bangi Pas Youth votes to cut ties with Bersatu
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bangi Pas Youth passed a motion to cut political ties with Bersatu in its annual general meeting today. "Bangi Pas Youth unanimously condemn in the strongest terms a statement made by Bersatu secretary-general (Datuk Seri) Mohamed Azmin Ali against the Pas president during Bersatu's machinery launch in Melaka," the post added. Azmin had previously taken aim at Pas and said during a ceramah that Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang should "open his eyes" and take a look at Bersatu's election machinery.

items.title: Man, 42, found dead nearly a week after going missing in Batu Kurau
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — A man with mental disabilities who was reported missing after leaving his home in Simpang 4, Anak Kurau, near Batu Kurau last Sunday was found dead in a plantation area near Teratak Afrina Chalet today. Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said the department received an emergency call on the discovery of the body of Zakaria Shamsudin, 42, at 12.19 pm. On June 1, Zakaria was reported missing after leaving his home at about 4 pm.

items.title: Govt makes recycling facilities compulsory at shopping malls to boost waste management
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — The government has made the provision of Recycling Facilities (FPS) at shopping malls mandatory for the application and renewal of business premises licences under local authorities (PBT), to be implemented in phases starting this month. The Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) in a statement today said the matter was agreed upon during the Cabinet meeting yesterday. KPKT said the decision to mandate FPS is a strategic move by the government to strengthen national solid waste management, facilitate public access to recycling facilities, and accelerate the transition to a more sustainable circular economy.

items.title: GIIB founder tells High Court of 'corporate mafia' plot, WhatsApp conspiracy and gun threat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: GIIB Holdings Bhd founder Tai Boon Wee told the High Court that he was targeted in a "corporate mafia" takeover attempt allegedly coordinated through a WhatsApp group called "soldier boys", and was also threatened with a gun at a restaurant. Under examination-in-chief by lawyer Marcus Liew, Tai said his introduction to Yee and Wong in 2020 sparked a shareholding dispute and an alleged smear campaign aimed at driving down GIIB's share price. Tai added that the group also discussed delaying banking processes, manipulating media coverage and issuing negative statements to depress the company's share price.

items.title: LPT2 crash update: Woman dies after leg amputation
items.summary: DUNGUN: A woman who suffered severe injuries, including the amputation of her left leg, in a crash at Km341.5 of the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2) near the Paka Rest and Service Area (R&R) yesterday has died. Acting district police chief Deputy Superintendent Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said police were informed that Maziah Che Hassan, 57, died while receiving treatment at Dungun Hospital at 7.15pm yesterday. Maziah was travelling in a Proton X70 sport utility vehicle (SUV) with four others when the accident occurred around 5.15pm.

items.title: India's 'Cockroach' youth movement founder arrives in New Delhi to protest Modi
items.summary: NEW DELHI: The founder of India's viral Cockroach Janta Party arrived in New Delhi on Saturday to lead a protest against Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government, taking the country's largest online youth movement to the streets for the first time. Dozens of police officers gathered near Jantar Mantar in central New Delhi on Saturday, barricading some of the surrounding roads as protesters shouted slogans demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Modi's government has blocked the movement's X account in the country, a move the Cockroach Janta Party has challenged in a Delhi court.

items.title: GTPC launched as national TVET talent platform to bridge industry and graduates, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today officially launched the GITC TVET Placement Centre (GTPC), the nation’s first integrated national talent placement platform aimed at bridging the gap between industry and skilled talent. The platform, launched in conjunction with the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here will serve as a structured ecosystem connecting students, graduates, institutions and industry to facilitate industrial training, apprenticeships and employment opportunities. Ahmad Zahid, who is also the National TVET Council chairman, said National TVET Day now symbolises the central role of TVET in Malaysia’s development agenda.

items.title: PDRM begins early preparations to ensure smooth conduct of Johor state election, says Saifuddin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) have begun initial preparations to ensure that the 16th Johor state election runs smoothly. “Insya-Allah, with careful planning and cooperation of all parties, the Johor state election can be run smoothly, peacefully and in harmony for the people of Johor,” he posted on Facebook today after visiting the Batu Pahat district police headquarters for a briefing on the security forces level of readiness and preparations for the Johor state election. The Election Commission announced that a special meeting for the Johor state election will be held on June 12, following the dissolution of the Johor state assembly on June 1 after the Johor Regent, Tunku Mahkota Ismail gave his consent.

items.title: Chow: Penang Mutiara Line LRT station designs nearing approval
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Station designs for the Penang Mutiara Line light rail transit (LRT) project are in the final stage of approval by the Penang Island City Council (MBPP), with the green light expected soon. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said construction at the Bandar Sri Pinang and Penang International Airport (PIA) stations was progressing well, making them the most advanced sites along the alignment since the project's groundbreaking in January last year. At the PIA station, Chow said construction of station piers and the viaduct was progressing steadily, with five piers already taking shape.

items.title: Children fishing in drain discover man's body
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR: A group of children fishing in a roadside drain made a grim discovery after finding the body of a man along Jalan Merbau Kudung here today. The victim's nephew, who wished to be known only as Mohd, said he was informed by villagers and rushed to the scene at noon. He said his uncle's body was found some 500m from his home in Kampung Nyior Sebatang.

items.title: 'I thought I would perish': Everest survivor recounts ordeal
items.summary: KATHMANDU: A Nepali mountaineer who survived nearly a week on Mount Everest said he "chewed ice" to stay alive, as he recovered in a hospital after a miraculous rescue that stunned the climbing community. Left stranded in freezing temperatures near Everest's "death zone", where oxygen levels are critically low, Dawa Sherpa said he survived for days with almost no food or water. I chewed the ice hard," he said.

items.title: Ayob Khan: IPCC under Home Ministry gives public independent avenue to report police misconduct
items.summary: KUCHING, June 6 — The Independent Police Conduct Commission (IPCC) provides an alternative platform for the public to lodge complaints related to police misconduct, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said. He said that previously, most complaints involving police misconduct were handled by the Royal Malaysia Police’s (PDRM) Integrity and Standards Compliance Department (JIPS). It aims to enhance integrity within the police force, investigate misconduct involving PDRM personnel and recommend disciplinary action.

items.title: Damansara Uptown peeping tom case: NFA after victim withdraws report
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: Police have classified the investigation into an alleged peeping tom incident at a fast food outlet here as no further action (NFA). Petaling Jaya district police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsudin Mamat said the case was resolved after both the complainant and the suspect attended the police station, where the woman subsequently withdrew her report. He said the investigation papers were later referred to the deputy public prosecutor (DPP) for review.

items.title: Hamzah dismisses reports linking him to Parti Cinta Malaysia takeover
items.summary: On the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections, Hamzah said political cooperation with Pas would be one of the main pillars in facing the polls, with discussions focused on safeguarding the people's interests and future development. The former Bersatu deputy president said close ties with Pas would be used to shape a political approach aimed at ensuring stability, prosperity and the wellbeing of the people in both states. Hamzah said he and Pas would continue to defend the interests of the people and oppose any party seen as undermining stability in Johor and Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Zahid calls for separate accreditation body to oversee TVET sector
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 (Bernama) -- Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has proposed the establishment of a dedicated accreditation body for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) to coordinate the assessment and recognition of the field without affecting the roles of the Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) and the Department of Skills Development (JPK). Ahmad Zahid, who is also chairman of the National TVET Council, said the proposal was made in view of the current system, which separates academic and skills-based accreditation, whereas TVET requires a dedicated body to provide more comprehensive coordination. He added that he had discussed the matter with Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman, Higher Education Ministry secretary-general Datuk Dr Aminuddin Hassim and, prior to that, with Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir.

items.title: Taiwan says Chinese coast guard, research ships near key S. China Sea islands
items.summary: TAIPEI: Taiwan said on Saturday that a Chinese coast guard ship and a survey ship had carried out the first coordinated operation to "provoke" Taiwan in waters around strategically located islands in the South China Sea. Taiwan's coast guard said in a statement that along with a Chinese coast guard ship that had approached the Pratas on Friday, a Chinese oceanographic survey vessel approached the islands on Saturday. "This is the first observed instance of Chinese coast guard and survey vessels acting in coordination to provoke Taiwan," it said.

items.title: Chow Kon Yeow: George Town better equipped to preserve Unesco heritage status after 18 years
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 6 — After nearly two decades of being recognised as a Unesco World Heritage Site, Penang is now in a stronger position to manage and preserve George Town due to various efforts undertaken since the recognition on July 7, 2008. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the 18-year journey was not easy as the state government faced various challenges, especially in the first five years after George Town was recognised as a World Heritage Site. He said this at a press conference for the organisation of the George Town Heritage Celebrations (GTHC) 2026 in conjunction with the celebration of George Town’s recognition as a UNESCO World Heritage Site here today.

items.title: Shark attack kills man off Western Australia coast
items.summary: SYDNEY: A man has died after being attacked by a shark while fishing off the coast of Western Australia state, police said on Saturday, the third fatal shark attack in Australia in three weeks. The 35-year-old was attacked on Saturday morning while spearfishing with family off the state's south coast at Michaelmas Island, close to the town of Albany, about 388 km (241 miles) south of state capital Perth, police said. A 4.5-metre (14.8-foot) shark, of an unknown species, was spotted by a member of the public near Michaelmas Island, a nature reserve that receives few visitors, on Saturday, state authorities said.

items.title: Penang Mutiara Line LRT stations taking shape as approval process nears completion, says CM
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 6 — Construction of stations for the Mutiara Line Light Rail Transit (LRT) project is progressing steadily, with station designs currently awaiting approval from the Penang Island City Council (MBPP), which is expected soon. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said pier and viaduct works for the Penang International Airport (LTAPP) Station are underway, with five piers already completed. At Bandar Sri Pinang Station, foundation works and station columns have been completed, with the project set to enter its main construction phase soon.

items.title: Rescuers halt search for two last men lost in Laos cave
items.summary: VIENTIANE: Rescuers called off their search Saturday for two men trapped in a semi-submerged cave in Laos for more than two weeks, as the site became unstable and survival hopes faded. The two others remained missing despite intensive searching by Laos and international rescue teams. "The water in the cave was already manageable, but the cave entrance started to become unstable."

items.title: Titiwangsa’s Tropical Fruit Pavilion opens as centrepiece of KL Architecture Festival 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming today launched the Tropical Fruit Pavilion at Titiwangsa Park, a key attraction of the year-long Kuala Lumpur Architecture Festival 2026 (KLAF 2026). “The pavilion today and also KLAF, it represents a distinctive and thoughtful contribution. Rooted in Malaysia’s tropical heritage and seasonal landscape, the Tropical Fruit Pavilion translates cultural references into spatial installations that explore identity, ecology and public life through architecture.

items.title: Malaysia taps US, Africa and Australia to secure national energy supply
items.summary: PETALING JAYA: The government is strengthening Malaysia's energy security by diversifying its supply sources to ensure adequate energy availability and minimise risks arising from disruptions in supplier countries, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said. He said the government, through Petronas and oil companies, remained committed to securing new sources of supply, including from South Africa and the United States, to ensure economic activities continue uninterrupted. "For coal, Malaysia continues to depend largely on Indonesia while expanding its supply sources to other countries, including the United States and Australia.

items.title: Woman with severed leg in Proton X70 crash on LPT2 dies at Dungun Hospital
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 6 — A woman who suffered a severed left leg in an accident at Kilometre 341.5 of the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2) near the Paka Rest and Service Area (R&R) here yesterday died while receiving treatment at Dungun Hospital last night. Dungun Acting Police Chief DSP Jumaidy Bah Chong Weh said police received a report that Maziah Che Hassan, 57, died at 7.15pm. “As a result of the impact, the vehicle spun several times and became lodged on the road barrier,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Search for missing hiker Jaslinda enters Day 14 with no new leads
items.summary: IPOH: The search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, has entered its 14th day without any new leads, although search efforts have continued on a smaller scale since Thursday. Perak Fire and Rescue Department Operations Division assistant director Sabarodzi Nor Ahmad said several items had been recovered from riverbanks, hill slopes and search routes throughout the operation, but none were found to belong to the victim. "Yesterday, five search teams comprising personnel from the Forestry Department and MGP entered the search sectors.

items.title: BN aims to wrest Johor Jaya from DAP after 13 years
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: Barisan Nasional (BN) is aiming to recapture Johor Jaya from DAP in the coming state election, an urban seat it last won 13 years ago. Johor BN Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the coalition was also determined to retain Permas. Noor Azleen, who is also Pasir Gudang Umno Youth chief, said these were among the key issues discussed at the Pasir Gudang BN parliamentary meeting last night.

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in Jalan Reko crash
items.summary: District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said the incident occurred about 11.13pm last night. "The motorcyclist, a local technician, was travelling from Putrajaya towards his home in Semenyih when the incident happened. Naazron said the motorcyclist was confirmed dead at the scene due to the severity of the impact.

items.title: Housing Ministry revives over 1,500 sick and abandoned projects worth RM148b, says Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — More than 1,500 sick and abandoned private housing projects nationwide have been successfully revived by the Ministry of Housing and Local Government’s (KPKT) special task force from 2023 to May this year. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the recovery efforts involved 1,576 projects comprising 188,525 housing units, with a total estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. He said the achievement reflected KPKT’s continued commitment to strengthening the nation’s housing sector through the Task Force on Sick and Abandoned Private Housing Projects (TFST), which was established in 2023.

items.title: Zahid proposes dedicated accreditation body for TVET institutions
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has proposed the establishment of a dedicated accreditation body for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions, separate from existing academic and skills accreditation agencies. Zahid, who is also National TVET Council chairman, said the move was necessary given the presence of 1,395 TVET institutions nationwide. He said several developed countries already operated separate accreditation bodies for technical education, a model Malaysia could emulate.

items.title: No sign of missing climber Jaslinda as Gunung Batu Putih search enters 14th day
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — The search and rescue (SAR) operation for Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing in the Gunung Batu Putih area, has entered its 14th day without any new leads, although the search has continued on a smaller scale since Thursday. He said that as of Thursday, eight members of the Special, Tactical and Rescue Team of Malaysia (STORM) and two Forest Mountain Guides (MGP) were still involved in the search sector location, and all of them safely exited at 11.30 am. “Yesterday, there were five search teams consisting of members from the Forestry Department and MGP who entered the search sector location.

items.title: Agong orders swift gazetting of KL green spaces, flood retention ponds, says Hannah Yeoh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has decreed that there should be no delay in gazetting Kuala Lumpur’s green spaces and flood retention ponds, Federal Territories Minister Hannah Yeoh said today. “The Yang di-Pertuan Agong decreed that we must not delay the decision on the gazetting of areas such as green zones and flood retention ponds, because these are areas of public interest. Separately, Yeoh said Kuala Lumpur already has sufficient infrastructure and that the current focus is on improving and revitalising public recreational spaces, particularly for families.

items.title: Senior cop misconduct cases on IPCC radar, warns deputy IGP
items.summary: KUCHING: Several integrity-related cases involving senior police officers ranked Senior Assistant Commissioner and above are now on the radar of the Independent Police Conduct Commission (IPCC). Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said he had received public complaints and that such cases be lodged with the IPCC. He said police officers and personnel must no longer assume they are only being monitored by the Integrity and Standard Compliance Department, as the Home Ministry also has a dedicated agency overseeing them.

items.title: Rafizi: Bersama to contest Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Bersama Malaysia leader Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli has confirmed that the party will contest in the upcoming Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections. Speaking to reporters after setting up a public complaints and party membership registration counter at the Pandan Indah market here this morning, Rafizi said the party will begin accepting applications from prospective candidates next Monday (June 8) as part of its selection process, Sin Chew Daily reported. When asked whether the three-week-old party would have sufficient time to prepare candidates for the elections, Rafizi said applications would open on Monday and all applicants would undergo interviews and assessments.

items.title: Smuggling bid sinks in RM57.15mil drug bust in Muar
items.summary: MUAR: Police have busted a syndicate believed to be smuggling drugs to a neighbouring country by sea, seizing narcotics worth RM57.15 million in a raid at Parit Amat Laut yesterday. Region Two Marine Police commander Assistant Commissioner Noor Azman Jamal said the operation was launched following intelligence on smuggling activities in the area. He said the raiding team spotted a boat and several vehicles at a coastal area off Muar shortly after midnight.

items.title: 1,576 sick, abandoned housing projects worth RM148bil revived since 2023
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has revived more than 1,576 sick and abandoned housing projects nationwide since 2023, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the recovered projects involved 188,525 housing units. Nga said this reflected the government's commitment to addressing longstanding issues involving stalled housing developments and protecting homebuyers affected by project delays.

items.title: Nga Kor Ming says 4,000 litterbugs now paying fines while sweeping Kuala Lumpur streets for free
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — As of today, over 4,000 litterbugs have been caught so far, said Housing and Local Government (KPKT) minister Nga Kor Ming. Nga said that the Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) now has 4,000 free social workers to help clean up the streets of KL. “KL is going to have not 1,000 but 10,000 facial recognition and high definition CCTV.

items.title: Amir Hamzah assures Sultan Abdullah of consistent federal aid and ECRL boost for Pahang
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 6 — Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan met with the Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri’ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, last week to provide clarification on federal allocations and assistance channelled to the state. He said the audience enabled him to give a comprehensive briefing covering federal allocations to Pahang, which have shown yearly increases, as well as focusing on priority projects proposed by the state government. “From the briefing, His Royal Highness was able to see that there is indeed a commitment from the federal government to assist states, including Pahang, through the implementation of development projects, as well as allocations for the state, which have been increasing consistently year by year.

items.title: Nga: KL ranked 65th in smart city index as skyscrapers soar and tourists flood in
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Housing and Local Government Ministry (KPKT) minister Nga Kor Ming revealed that Kuala Lumpur now ranks 65th in the global smart city index. Speaking at the launch of the KL Architecture Festival’s (KLAF) Tropical Fruit Pavilion launch at Titiwangsa Lake Garden this morning, Nga also said that KL is among the top 10 most popular cities in the world in terms of international tourist arrivals according to Mastercard. The launch was also attended by the Federal Territories minister Hannah Yeoh as well as KL mayor Datuk Fadlun Mak Ujud.

items.title: MACC launches AI-powered MACCAMPUS platform to boost anti-corruption education in universities
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 6 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today launched MACCAMPUS, a digital platform incorporating artificial intelligence (AI) and gamification to strengthen integrity education and anti-corruption awareness among tertiary students. MACC Chief Commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said the initiative reflects their commitment to engaging university students in enhancing integrity education, corruption prevention and positive values through smartphones and information technology applications. “To ensure MACC remains capable of engaging university students consistently and effectively, it requires a more innovative, interactive and technology-driven educational approach,” he said when speaking at the Integrity Financial Literacy @ Student Anti-Corruption Programme 2026 at Dewan Sultan Mizan, UMT, here.

items.title: Malaysia diversifying energy supply sources to ensure security, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 6 — The government is using a sound risk management approach by diversifying energy supply sources to ensure the country’s energy supply is sufficient, said Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan. He said the government, through Petroliam Nasional Bhd and oil companies, remains committed to seeking new supply sources, including from South Africa and the United States, to ensure the continuity of economic activities is not affected. “For coal, Malaysia continues to depend largely on Indonesia while expanding its supply sources to other countries, including the United States and Australia.

items.title: Fahmi Fadzil pays tribute to late Pahang PKR leader Abas Awang
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 6 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil has conveyed his condolences to the family of Pahang PKR leader Abas Awang, who died yesterday. In a Facebook post today, Fahmi said Abas’s contributions, sacrifices and service to the party and the people will always be remembered. Abas died at his residence in Section 4, Bandar Baru Bangi, Selangor.

items.title: Four suspected cable thieves caught on video along MRR2, police launch manhunt
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — Police are tracking four men suspected of stealing cables along the side of the Middle Ring Road 2 (MRR2) here after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Ampang Jaya District Police Chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said police detected a viral video at about 5 pm yesterday showing several individuals believed to be stealing cables from a manhole along the highway. Khairul Anuar said the complainant managed to record the incident before lodging the report and the case is being investigated under Section 431A of the Penal Code and Section 235 of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998.

items.title: MARA TVET institutions record 99.5pc graduate employability rate, says Zahid
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 (Bernama) -- A total of 270 Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) have recorded a graduate employability rate of 99.5 per cent, Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. He said the impressive achievement demonstrated that MARA’s TVET institutions are at the forefront of advancing the nation’s TVET agenda, which has now entered the TVET 2.0 phase. “They have embraced TVET 2.0 not only through the various contributions I mentioned earlier, but also by signing agreements with the National TVET Council and most TVET institutions across the country to ensure that all TVET students will be employed by the industry, with 100 per cent absorption into the workforce,” said Ahmad Zahid, who is also chairman of the National TVET Council, in his welcoming remarks at the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here today.

items.title: Anwar: Selected TVET students to receive AI and cryptology training under new govt initiative
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — All government training and research institutions will be required to provide training programmes in emerging fields for selected Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) students beginning July, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said at least 50 to 100 TVET students at each selected institution will be given early training opportunities in emerging and high-technology fields such as artificial intelligence (AI) and cryptology. Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, ministers and Shamsul Azri.

items.title: Anwar: Govt approves up to RM50m from PTPK fund to boost TVET 2.0 programme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 6 — The government has agreed to allow the use of up to RM50 million from the High-Impact Programme fund under the Skills Development Fund Corporation (PTPK) to strengthen the implementation of Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) 2.0, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the use of the fund is subject to conditions, namely that employers and industry players must work closely with the government to ensure the success of the TVET 2.0 agenda. This will also enhance training programmes and exposure opportunities,” he said when officiating the National TVET Day 2026 celebration here today.

items.title: Two-year petrol smuggling activity to Thailand uncovered in Tumpat raid, six detained
items.summary: TUMPAT, June 6 — Six individuals have been arrested following the discovery of an illegal petrol storage facility allegedly used to stockpile fuel for smuggling into Thailand in an operation that has been going on for about two years. Brigade Southeast PGA commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the operation was launched following intelligence on suspected fuel smuggling activities at a premises in Tualang, Pengkalan Kubor. He said surveillance conducted for about 30 minutes showed several four-wheel-drive vehicles, believed to be Mitsubishi Pajero units, entering the premises in a suspicious manner before enforcement teams moved in.

items.title: Mydin says essential goods supply in Malaysia remains stable despite global uncertainty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — The supply of essential goods in Malaysia remains stable despite ongoing uncertainties in global supply chains, says Mydin Mohamed Holdings Bhd (Mydin) managing director Datuk Dr Ameer Ali Mydin. Planting and harvesting activities are proceeding as usual, while fertiliser supplies remain sufficient. On imported goods, Ameer Ali said local retailers have adopted forward-buying strategies to ensure a consistent and adequate supply of essential imported products.

items.title: FamilyMart apologises, says delivery rider’s wellbeing attended to after viral soup incident
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 6 — FamilyMart Malaysia has apologised over an incident involving a delivery rider who was allegedly splashed with hot soup in a viral video, saying it has already attended to the rider’s wellbeing. FamilyMart Malaysia said the company has taken steps to ensure the delivery rider’s welfare is being addressed, while also cooperating fully with the authorities in their ongoing investigation. The incident surfaced after a 23-second video began circulating online on May 28 showing a heated exchange between a delivery rider and a FamilyMart employee.

items.title: Terrifying LPT2 crash leaves woman, 57, with severed leg, four others injured
items.summary: DUNGUN, June 6 — A woman lost her left leg after the car she was travelling in was involved in an accident at Kilometre 341.5 of the East Coast Expressway 2 (LPT2), near the Paka Rest and Service (R&R) area near here, yesterday. Operations Commander senior fire officer II Nor Hazamullah Abdullah said the 57-year-old woman suffered the injury when the Proton X70 sports utility vehicle (SUV) she was travelling in skidded off the road at the scene. “The victim, whose left leg was severed below the knee, was successfully extricated by firefighters.

items.title: Police tracking road bully after alleged helmet attack on car windscreen in Ipoh
items.summary: IPOH, June 6 — Police are tracking down a motorcyclist believed to have smashed a car windscreen with a helmet in an incident along Jalan Raja Ashman Shah here early yesterday morning. Ipoh district police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said police received a report from the victim, a local man, regarding the incident, which occurred at about 1.26 am. “Initial investigations found that the incident occurred after the victim honked at a motorcyclist who was riding erratically and nearly got in his way.
